_comment: Manual disambiguation for umbrella product names that resolve to multiple catalog records. Built in Round 12 from build_edges.py discard logs ('ambiguous-substring' and 'known-unresolvable: langchain/redis/...'). build_edges.py consults this table BEFORE falling back to its own ambiguity / unresolvable rules. Key matching is case-insensitive on the normalised hint (lowercased + whitespace-collapsed). 'primary_id' is the default catalog target. 'alternates' provides phrase-specific overrides — when the cell context contains one of the alternate phrases, the alternate id wins.

langchain.reason: Bare 'LangChain' mentions in adapter/backend cross-refs refer to the LangChain framework integration surface — that's the umbrella row. LangMem (the memory subsystem) is a distinct product with its own row.

langgraph.reason: Bare 'LangGraph' mentions in checkpointer/memory-adapter cross-refs default to the persistence (memory) row, which is the memory-tier framing. The orchestration row is for control-flow context.

mem0.reason: Bare 'Mem0' references the core managed memory product. The MCP server and OpenMemory rows are derivative surfaces.

redis.reason: No first-party Redis row in the catalog — it's a generic substrate. Mentions remain unresolvable but are documented here so future ingestion knows to add a Redis substrate row if needed.

claude.reason: Bare 'Claude' inside coding-agent / harness cell text most commonly refers to Claude Code (the harness). The Anthropic Claude Memory chat product, CLAUDE.md file pattern, and the auto-memory subsystem are distinct rows reached via more-specific phrasing.

langsmith.reason: LangSmith is listed in the cross-ref CSV but no first-party row exists in the catalog (LangChain ecosystem observability). Documented for future ingestion.

langgraph persistence.reason: Explicit phrase; ensures no fallback to orchestration.

github.reason: Bare 'GitHub' in coding-agent cell text (e.g. ChatGPT Codex desc 'integrates with GitHub') refers to the Copilot Agent Mode integration in nearly all cases. Workspace and custom-instructions are distinct named surfaces.

strands agents.reason: Bare 'Strands Agents' refers to the AWS Strands Agents product (the agent framework). The memory subsystem is a separate row.

livekit.reason: Bare 'LiveKit' in voice/realtime cell text refers to the LiveKit Agents framework — the canonical first-party row.

pipecat.reason: Bare 'Pipecat' is the Daily-stewarded open-source realtime voice framework.

gr00t.reason: Bare 'GR00T' refers to the umbrella NVIDIA GR00T/Isaac developer product. The N1.7 paper row is the specific model release.

physical intelligence.reason: Bare 'Physical Intelligence' (or π / pi-zero / π0) in robotics-FM context defaults to the π0.5 model row (the most recent canonical release). The company/org row is used when the mention is explicitly organisational.

mistral.reason: Bare 'Mistral' in embedding/memory-substrate cell text defaults to Mistral Embed (the embedding product). Agents API, Le Chat Memories, and Mistral Medium are reached via more-specific phrasing.

replit.reason: Bare 'Replit' in coding-agent cross-refs defaults to the most-recent Replit Agent v3 row. The original Replit Agent and replit.md spec are distinct rows reached via more-specific phrasing.

anthropic.reason: Bare 'Anthropic' in memory-product cross-refs defaults to Anthropic Claude Memory (the canonical first-party memory product). Claude Code, Computer Use, Managed Agents Memory, and Circuit Tracing are distinct rows reached via more-specific phrasing.

_skipped._comment: Umbrellas considered in Round 14 but not added because primary_id did not resolve in landscape.json.
_skipped.deepseek: No DeepSeek R1 product row exists in catalog (only engram-deepseek--arxiv-2601-07372, a research paper). Documented for future ingestion if DeepSeek R1 is added as a first-party row.
